_comment: Single source of truth for the build environment used for the Linux release binary. Images are pinned by digest (not a mutable :latest tag) so that any change to the actual bundled OS packages (openssl-libs, zlib, etc.) is a visible diff here. python_version is the distro Python package (dnf install python<version>) used on Linux, and also the actions/setup-python version used on Windows/macOS, so all three release binaries are built with the same interpreter version. release.yml's build job reads this file (image + python_version) to build each platform. Changing this file triggers scripts/refresh_manylinux_licenses.py via .github/workflows/manylinux-license-refresh.yml, which re-derives scripts/manylinux_licenses.json (and, transitively, THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES) from the new image's actual RPM packages.
